questions.question: At 483 K, the equilibrium constant Kp for a reaction is 4. If the temperature is increased, favoring the endothermic direction, what happens to Kp?
questions.solution: For endothermic reactions, increasing temperature increases the equilibrium constant.

questions.question: Which of the following alkyl halides undergoes SN1 reaction most rapidly under identical conditions? (Solvent: aqueous ethanol, 37°C)
questions.hint: SN1 mechanisms proceed via carbocation intermediates. The more stable the carbocation, the faster the reaction.
questions.solution: SN1 rate depends on carbocation stability (3° > 2° > 1°). Option A forms a highly stable tertiary carbocation.

questions.question: 46 g of a non-volatile solute (molar mass = 60 g/mol) is dissolved in 250 mL of water. Assuming ideal behaviour and solvent density of 1 g/mL, calculate the relative lowering of vapour pressure.
questions.solution: Moles solute = 46/60 = 0.767. Moles water = 250/18 = 13.89. Mole fraction = 0.05231.
